exits.label: ◂ back into the Night Market's crooked lanes

npcs.name: Vesper
npcs.title: the Collector — vivid with a hundred stolen selves
npcs.hail: *(Everything in the grey is grey — except her. She sits in a chair that shouldn't exist among the cold cairns, poured into color and appetite and *presence*, more alive than a living thing, and it takes your dead-touched eye only a moment to see why: she is wearing other people. A gesture that was someone's mother's. A laugh cut from a girl who drowned. A hundred small brilliances, each lifted whole from a soul now faded to a smudge. She looks up at you with delight so warm and so genuine that the cold part of you leans toward it before the rest of you can object.)* …*oh*, at *last.* something *interesting* wanders in. *(she sets down a glass of something that isn't wine and shouldn't be here)* …you're the *Returned*, aren't you. the one who *crosses.* …*(she smiles, and it is a wonderful smile, and it is stitched from four different people)* …*sit.* i *collect* the *interesting*, cold one, and you are the *single* most interesting thing to *cross* my *threshold* in an *age.* …*(a delighted, conspiratorial lean)* …and you *look* — forgive me — you look *cold.* *fading* at the *edges* of your *feeling.* …*(silk)* …i can *help* with *that.* …i have *so* *much* *warmth* …just *lying* about.

npcs.name: Toller
npcs.title: a soul who gave Vesper his best pieces, and can't remember why
npcs.hail: *(Near the edge of her bright little court, a soul stands who is going the way she is vivid — thinned, smudged, a person with the most interesting parts rubbed out. He watches Vesper with a devotion that has outlasted the memories that earned it, the way a dog waits at a grave.)* …*(his voice is faint, worn)* …isn't she — isn't she *marvelous.* …*(he says it with the automatic warmth of a thing said ten thousand times)* …i *gave* her — i *think* i gave her — *(he frowns, reaching for something that isn't there anymore)* …i had a — there was a *day*, i think, a *good* one, the *best* one, and i — she *admired* it *so*, and i *wanted* her to *have* it, because she makes *everything* she *holds* so — …*(he trails off, the thread gone)* …i can't — what *was* it? the *day* i gave her? …*(a flicker of something like fear, quickly smoothed over)* …*no* matter. she *keeps* it *safe.* she keeps *all* my *best* bits *safe.* …*safer* than *i* ever — …*(he smiles, emptily)* …isn't she *marvelous.*

variants.text: *(Her eyes travel over you and light with a collector's pure covetous joy, and she leans in as though you and she are the only two real things in a room full of grey.)* …*oh*, but you're *further* *gone* than i *thought.* …*(gentle, delighted, terrible)* …look at you. the *cold's* halfway up your *arms* already, isn't it — the *feeling* going *out* of you *hour* by *hour*, the *names* you've *kept* starting to *weigh* less, the *faces* starting to *blur.* …*(she purrs)* …i *know* that *chill*, cold one. i *felt* it *creeping* up *me*, *once*, a *long* time ago — and then i *learned* the *secret* the *whole* grey is *too* *frightened* to *say* *aloud:* …you don't have to *fade.* …you can *fill* the *cold* with *someone* *else's* *warmth.* …i have *cabinets* of it. …*(she offers her hand, and the offer is the most tempting thing you have felt since you died)* …*one* *taste*, cold one. …just to *remember* …what *warm* *feels* like.

*(She marks the bare place where no god sits on you, and her delight sharpens into something hungrier.)* …*Unclaimed.* …*(she savors it)* …oh, you're my *favorite* *vintage*, cold one. the *godless.* …*(silk)* …do you know *why?* because when the *claimed* fade, some *god* somewhere *notices* the *hole.* keeps a *little* *candle.* …but the *Unclaimed* — *(she spreads her hands, all sympathy)* …when *you* fade, cold one, *no* one *marks* it. *no* one *misses* the *warmth* that *goes.* …*(the warmth of her smile is total and it is a trap)* …so it may as well go *somewhere* it's *treasured.* …to *someone* who'll *wear* it *proudly* …instead of the *Wall*, which will just *grind* it to *nothing.* …*sit.* let me *show* you the *kinder* *ledger.*

*(She studies you with frank, luxurious interest, a connoisseur presented with an unfamiliar and promising vintage.)* …*mm.* …*whole*, aren't you. *undissolved.* *most* who cross my *threshold* are half-*smudge* already, and you're — *(she tilts her head)* …*intact.* how *rare.* how *delicious.* …*(she laughs, and it is warm and real and stolen)* …don't *bristle*, cold one, i'm not going to *take* anything you don't *hand* me — that's the *whole* *art* of it, the *handing.* …*(she gestures at her bright, appalling court)* …*everyone* here *gave.* *gladly.* …*sit*, and let me *tell* you the *only* *philosophy* in the *grey* that *isn't* just *waiting* to *lose.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(She talks the way a great fire draws a cold room — you know exactly what she is and you lean in anyway, and she knows you know, and that is the delicious part for her.)* …the *grey* has *one* *rule*, cold one, and *everyone* *pretends* not to *know* it: *everything* *fades.* every *soul* here is *losing* itself, *grain* by *grain*, to a *Wall* that *eats* the *forgotten.* …*(she swirls the glass)* …*most* just …*let* it. *drift* and *dim* and *dissolve*, *noble* and *doomed.* …*(she leans in, eyes bright)* …*i* *declined.* i *looked* at the *arithmetic* and i *saw* the *loophole:* a *self* that *fades* can be *replaced* …with *pieces* of *selves* that *haven't.* a *laugh* here, a *love* there, a *best* *day* *lifted* whole from a soul too *tired* to *keep* it. …*(she smiles her four-souled smile)* …i am *more* *alive* than *anything* in this *dead* place, cold one, and i did it by *refusing* to *fade.* …*is* that a *crime? …or* is it the *only* *sane* *response* …to a *cosmos* that *steals* *you* *anyway?*

choices.text: *(read the Collector — past the borrowed brilliance, to whatever is or isn't underneath)* "Hold still a moment, Vesper. Let me look at you — at what's actually there, under the hundred coats of other people."

*(the one who crosses, who owes her nothing)* "I'll tell you what I am, Vesper, since you collect the interesting: I'm the one soul the Wall gave back. Which means I've seen what's on the far side of the fading — and I know something you've spent a very long time not letting yourself notice. Careful what you offer to hand me. I might hand you something back."
*(the cold in you leans toward the warmth — take a taste)* "...One taste. You said one taste. I'm so tired of being cold, Vesper. Show me what you're offering."
"It's theft, Vesper, dressed in a smile. Every warm thing on you is a hole in someone else. That's not a loophole in the Wall — it's a Wall you built yourself, one stolen day at a time."

*(the thing that could break her: make her hold, for one second, the specific grief inside a single stolen memory — the weight she's spent an age refusing to feel)* "Here's what you've never let yourself notice, Vesper. You don't have a single self of your own — you gave up fading by giving up ever again feeling anything as *yours.* Every warmth you wear is numb to you, because it isn't yours to feel. So let me hand *you* something. That laugh you're wearing — it belonged to a girl who drowned at nineteen, mid-laugh, and it was the last sound her mother ever heard her make. Feel it. Just once. Feel whose it is."
"Let me walk your little court a moment, Vesper, before I decide what to make of you."

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(She permits the look, amused, certain there is nothing you can find that she has not already turned into an ornament — and your sense goes past the hundred stolen brilliances to the place they are hung.)* *(And you find nothing there. Not a wound, not a villain's cold calculation — an absence, a hollow the exact shape of a person, around which a hundred borrowed selves are arranged like coats on a coat-rack that is not, itself, a body. Your sense reads the truth she has built her whole vivid existence to outrun: that in the very act of refusing to fade, she faded completely. That the first self she spent — to buy the second — was her own, and she has been so busy wearing other people that she has not been anyone for a very long time. And your sense reads the exquisite cruelty of her cleverness turned on herself: that a stolen warmth can be worn but never felt, because feeling requires it to be yours, and nothing she wears is hers. She is the coldest soul in the grey, colder than the fading, colder than the Wall — a hollow at the center of a bonfire of other people's lives, warm to every eye but her own. And your sense reads the one thing that could reach her, and it is terrible, because it is also the one thing she has armored against most totally: to be made to feel, for a single unbearable instant, the specific grief that clings to just one of her stolen pieces — to hold whose it was, and what it cost them, and to understand that she is not adorned with a hundred warmths but haunted by a hundred griefs she has refused to feel. She could not survive feeling even one. And underneath the hollow, so faint it is nearly gone, your sense finds the last unspent grain of the person she was before she started spending herself: still there, still hers, still cold and small and waiting, the one thing in all her hoard she has never dared to wear, because it is the only thing that is actually her, and it is grieving, and she cannot bear it.)*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(For just a breath the delight flickers — not fear, she is far too grand for fear, but the wariness of a collector who has just been told a piece might collect *her.*)* …*hand* me something *back.* …*(she recovers, laughing, and the laugh is a drowned girl's)* …*oh*, i *do* like you. …*most* of what crosses my *threshold* *wants* something — *warmth*, *company*, a *taste* of *vividness* — and you walk in and *threaten* to *give* me a *gift.* …*(she leans back, luxuriant, but her eyes have sharpened)* …*careful*, cold one. i have *worn* the *cleverness* of *forty* *clever* *souls*, and *out*-*talked* the *lot* of them. …*(silk over steel)* …but *do* go *on.* what *could* the *one* who *crosses* *possibly* *have* …that a soul with a *hundred* *lives* …hasn't *already* *worn?*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(She takes your hand, and the warmth pours in — and gods, it is *everything*, a summer you never had, a love that was someone else's, a whole bright afternoon of being alive and unafraid — and for one long shameful moment the cold in you recedes and you understand, completely, why the smudged souls in her court gave and gave.)* …*there.* …*(she watches your face with a lover's satisfaction)* …*feel* that? that's a *fisherman's* *daughter's* *wedding* *morning.* she gave it to me for the *warmth* of my *company*, and now *you've* had a *sip.* …*(gently, terribly)* …it *fades*, of course. a *taste* always *fades* — you'll be *colder* in an *hour* than *before*, that's the — that's the *little* *catch.* …*(she smiles)* …but i have *cabinets* more, cold one. *cabinets* and *cabinets.* …you could *stay.* …you could be *warm* *whenever* the *cold* *bites*, for as *long* as the *hoard* *lasts.* …*(she strokes the back of your hand)* …*all* you'd have to *give* me …in *trade* …is the *one* thing you've *got* that i *don't:* …a *way* *out* of the *grey.* …the *crossing.* …*teach* me *that* …and the *cabinets* are *yours.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(She does not flinch — she applauds, softly, delighted to be seen and utterly unthreatened by it.)* …*theft.* …*(she tastes the word)* …*yes.* *obviously.* …did you think i didn't *know*, cold one? …*(she leans forward, and for the first time the charm has an edge you can cut yourself on)* …let me tell you about *theft.* the *Wall* *steals* *every* soul here, *whole*, for *nothing*, and calls it *justice.* the *gods* *steal* your *worship* and give back a *grey* *eternity.* *time* *steals* your *face.* …*everything* in this cosmos is *theft*, cold one, *top* to *bottom* — the *only* *question* is whether you're the *robbed* …or the *one* who *robbed* *back.* …*(she settles, serene)* …i looked at a *rigged* *game* and i *cheated* it *blind.* my *court* isn't *robbed*, cold one — they're *invested.* their *best* *days* live *on*, in *me*, *vivid*, instead of *rotting* in a *Wall.* …*(a glittering smile)* …i'm not a *thief.* i'm the *only* *honest* *banker* …in a *cosmos* of *thieves.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(You don't argue her philosophy — you can't win it, she's worn forty philosophers. You do the one thing she has no armor for: you take the drowned girl's laugh she's wearing and you hand her, gently, relentlessly, *whose it was* — the mother on the shore, the mid-laugh, the last sound — and you make her *hold* it.)* …*(the delight goes out of her face like a candle pinched)* …*(she tries to laugh — the drowned girl's laugh — and it comes out wrong, because for the first time in an age she can *hear* whose it is)* …*stop.* …*(barely)* …*stop* it, i — …*(she presses a hand to her mouth, to the borrowed laugh, and something breaks behind her eyes, a hundred refused griefs pressing at a door she has held shut for centuries)* …*nineteen.* …her *mother* was *on* the *shore.* …i *never* — i don't *feel* them, that's the *whole* — i *wear* them, i don't *feel* them, you're not *supposed* to *feel* — …*(and she feels it, one whole unbearable grief, and it is like watching a statue learn it has nerves)* …*(she is weeping, and she does not know how, the tears as unfamiliar to her as her own name)* …what have you — …*(she looks at her hands, at the hundred coats, and sees them, for one terrible instant, as a hundred *people*)* …*i'm* *not* — there's *nothing* *under* — …*(the smallest, most naked voice, nothing grand left in it)* …i *spent* *me* *first*, didn't i. …the *very* *first* *coin.* …i've been *cold* the *whole* — under *all* of it, i've been the *coldest* — …*(she looks at you, unmade)* …*how* do i — cold one, *how* do i *give* them *back?* …i don't *remember* how to be *anyone* …*small* enough …to *fit* *back* *into* *myself.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(You find the exact grief and you place it in her with surgical, merciless tenderness, and a soul who has worn a hundred lives and felt none of them feels, all at once, the whole weight of the first — and the bonfire of stolen selves does not just gutter, it *collapses inward*, and what's left when it falls is so small you almost can't see it.)* …*(the drowned girl's laugh dies in her throat and does not come back)* …*(she is very still, and then she begins, soundlessly, to give them back — you watch it happen, a warmth leaving her and drifting toward the smudged soul it was stolen from, and another, and another, the court around her flickering back toward personhood as she *empties*)* …*(with each one she diminishes, grows greyer, colder, more faded — and more *real*)* …*(and when the last stolen warmth has gone home, what remains is a small, grey, unremarkable soul, faded nearly to nothing, sitting in a chair that no longer shouldn't exist, and she is crying, and it is, at last, entirely her own grief she is crying)* …*(she looks up at you, and there is nothing marvelous left, and it is the first true thing she has been in centuries)* …*Vesper.* …*(she says her own name like meeting a stranger)* …that's — that was *my* name. before i *spent* it. …*(she is fading even as you watch, the borrowed vividness gone)* …*i'm* going to *dissolve* now, cold one. *properly.* the way i *cheated* for so — …*(a small, wet, astonished smile)* …*good.* …i'd *rather* fade as *one* *cold* *nobody* …who *gave* it all *back* …than *blaze* *forever* …as a *hundred* *stolen* *strangers.* …*(going)* …*thank* you …for *handing* me …the *one* thing …i could never *steal.* …*myself.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(You reach for the drowned girl's grief but you present it like an accusation, and a soul who has out-argued forty clever souls simply files it with the rest.)* …*(she lets the laugh finish, unbothered)* …*oh*, that's a *good* one. the *dead* *girl*, the *shore*, the *mother.* …*(she inclines her head, connoisseur to connoisseur)* …you're *trying* to make me *feel* it. …*(she smiles, and it does not reach the hollow you now suspect is there)* …*cleverer* souls than you have *tried* to *guilt* me *whole* again, cold one. it *doesn't* *take* — because to feel the *grief* in a *piece*, i'd have to feel it as *mine*, and *nothing* i *wear* is *mine*, that's the *whole* *architecture.* …*(and yet — just for an instant — her hand strays to the borrowed laugh at her throat, and stills there, and she notices herself doing it, and stops)* …*(quietly, and the charm has thinned)* …*come* *back*, cold one. …and next time …*don't* *hand* me the *grief* like a *verdict.* …*hand* it to me …like it's *mine* to *hold.* …*(she recovers her smile, but it is a beat too late)* …*(…that's the only way it would ever get past the door. not that i'd know. not that i've ever felt the door. …go.)*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(The grandeur reasserts itself, but there is a hairline crack in the lacquer now, and you both can see it.)* …*(she swirls the glass that isn't wine, and for once does not drink)* …you *rattled* something, cold one. …a *thing* i *keep* very *still* and *very* *deep*, under *all* the *lovely* *coats.* …*(she almost sounds curious about it)* …*nobody's* *reached* it in — i don't *know* how long. i *stopped* *counting* when i stopped being *anyone* who *counts.* …*(she looks at her court, her hundred stolen warmths, with something that isn't quite the old satisfaction)* …*go* on. *walk* my *little* *court.* *talk* to *Toller*, if his *thread* will *hold.* …and *come* *back*, cold one. …*(quieter)* …i find i *want* to know …what *else* …you can *hand* me. …*nobody's* *given* me anything …in a *very* …*long* …*time.*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(You overplay it — go grand, theatrical, *behold the emptiness beneath your hoard* — and a soul who invented theatricality recognizes a lesser performer and closes the theatre.)* …*(she claps, twice, slow)* …*bravo.* …the *hollow* *queen*, the *bonfire* of the *stolen*, the *coldest* *soul* in the *grey.* …*(she stands, and for the first time you feel the full weight of the forty lives she wears, and it is genuinely frightening)* …you *reached* for something *true*, cold one, and then you *dressed* it up *bigger* than it *was*, and *that* i *know* how to *survive*, because *bigness* is *armor* and i am *wearing* a *hundred* *suits* of it. …*(cold, magnificent, closed)* …*run* *along.* pester *Toller.* free a *smudge* or *two* if it *warms* you. …*(and then, as you turn, so low you might have imagined it)* …*(…you almost had it. the small true thing. and you made it loud. and loud is the one language i speak fluently and feel nothing in. come back quiet, cold one. or don't come back at all.)*

nodes.speaker: Vesper
nodes.text: *(She waves you off with a gracious, glittering hand, entirely unthreatened, a great fire certain of its own heat.)* …*by* *all* *means.* *inspect* the *merchandise.* …*(a knowing smile)* …*talk* to *Toller.* he'll *tell* you how *marvelous* i am — they *all* do, right up until the *thread* runs *out.* …*(she reclines)* …and when you've *seen* that *every* soul here *gave* *gladly*, and *smiled* while they *did* it …*come* *back*, cold one, and *tell* me *honestly* …*(silk)* …which of *us* …is *really* the *Wall.* …*me*, who *keeps* their *best* *days* *warm* and *worn* …or the *cosmos* …that would have *ground* them …to *nothing.*

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(Up close the smudging is worse — whole stretches of him gone vague, like a page left in the rain, and yet the devotion to Vesper glows steady where the rest of him has thinned.)* …you're *talking* to *her.* *Vesper.* …*(a worn, warm smile)* …isn't she — *(he reaches for 'marvelous' and this time, looking at you, something snags)* …i *give* her things. i *know* i *give* her *things.* the — the *best* *things.* …*(he frowns)* …but i can't — when i *try* to *remember* *what*, there's just a — a *warm* *place* where the *memory* *was*, like a *chair* somebody *just* *stood* up *from.* …*(he touches his own chest, puzzled)* …i had a *daughter.* i *think.* i can't — i can't *see* her *face* anymore. …*(quickly, defending against his own doubt)* …but that's *alright*, because *Vesper* *keeps* her *safe.* she keeps my *best* *bits* *safe.* …*(the emptiness flickers)* …*doesn't* she? …*she* *does*, doesn't she?

choices.text: *(read the given-away man — gently, to find what's left and what's recoverable)* "Let me look at you, Toller. At what's still yours. There's more left than you think."

*(the one who crosses)* "I'm the soul the Wall gave back, Toller — I see what's taken and what's only misplaced. She didn't keep your daughter safe. She's *wearing* her. That warm place in your chest isn't a thing you gave away gladly — it's a wound with a smile drawn over it. Let me show you."
"She isn't keeping them safe, Toller. She's spending them. Every 'best bit' you gave her is a hole in you she wears as a jewel. Your daughter's face isn't safe — it's on *her.*"
"What was she — your daughter? Reach for one thing. Not the face. A sound. A weight. Anything."
"Rest a moment, Toller. I'll come back for you."

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(He lets you look, trusting, and your sense moves into the rained-on page of him and finds, under the smudging, the shape of what was taken and — crucially — that it is not gone, only moved.)* *(And you find a father. That much survives everything: the fact of a daughter, load-bearing, the beam the whole house of him was built on, even now that the rooms have washed away. Your sense reads how Vesper worked him — not by force, never by force, that isn't the art; she admired. She sat with a lonely fading soul and admired his best day, his daughter's wedding, his proudest hour, admired it so warmly and so completely that giving it to her felt like the memory finally mattering to someone, felt like love, felt like being seen — and he handed it over glowing, and she wore it, and the warm place where it had been felt, for a while, like generosity instead of loss. And your sense reads the mechanism of the ruin: that each thing given made him a little vaguer, a little easier to talk out of the next thing, until the man who could have said no had himself been given away. But your sense reads the mercy in it too, the thing that matters: the memories are not destroyed. They are worn. They are ten feet away, on her, intact — the daughter's face, the wedding, all of it — and a soul who walks both ways, who can hand things across, could hand them *back*, if the wearer could be made to loosen her grip. He is not erased. He is robbed. And the robbed, unlike the erased, can be repaid.)*

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(The word *wearing* lands somewhere the devotion doesn't cover, and you watch a terrible doubt open in him like a crack in ice.)* …*wearing* her. …*(his hand goes to his chest, to the warm empty place)* …*no*, she — she *keeps* — …*(but the doubt is in now, and it is spreading)* …she has a — Vesper has a *laugh* she does, a *lovely* one, and i always thought — i thought it *reminded* me of — *(his faded eyes widen)* …it's — it's *my* — that's my *daughter's* — …*(the smile he's worn for an age cracks and underneath it is a grief so old it has petrified)* …she's *wearing* my *girl's* *laugh.* …and *smiling* at me *with* it. …and i've been *standing* *here* …*thanking* her. …*(he looks at you, unmoored, terrified, awake)* …*(barely)* …can you — you said you *hand* things *across.* …*(the first want he's had in an age)* …can you get her *back? …my* *girl?* …*before* i *forget* i even had one …to *get* *back?*

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(He takes it hard — the truth is a cruelty and a rescue at once — and for a moment the devotion fights the fact, and then the fact wins, because some part of him has always known.)* …*a* *hole* i *wear* as a — *(he presses his hand flat to his chest)* …*(quietly, terribly)* …i *knew.* …*somewhere* *under* the *warm* i *knew*, and i *called* it *gratitude* because *gratitude* didn't *hurt* and the *knowing* did. …*(he looks at Vesper across her bright court, and the adoration curdles into something clearer)* …she *sat* with me when i was *fading* and *lonely* and she made me *feel* *seen*, and i'd have given her — i *did* give her — *anything.* …*(his faded face hardens)* …*my* *daughter's* *face.* i gave away my *daughter's* *face* …for the *warmth* of being *admired* by a *woman* who was *only* ever *admiring* the *jewel* she meant to *pry* *loose.* …*(he turns to you)* …*get* it *back*, cold one. …i don't *care* what it *costs* what's *left* of *me.* …a *father* who *forgot* his *daughter's* *face* …still *knows* …it's the *one* thing …he's *not* *allowed* to *lose.*

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(He tries — you watch him reach into the rained-on place with everything he has left — and the reaching itself is unbearable to witness.)* …a *sound.* …*(he closes his faded eyes)* …not the *face*, the face is — the face is *gone* to the *warm* place. but a — …*(straining)* …*small* *shoes.* …on *stone.* *running.* …*(his breath catches)* …*running* to the *door* when i — when i *came* *home.* the *sound* of *small* *shoes* *running* on *stone* and a — a *voice*, too *high* to *make* *words* yet, just — just *glad.* *glad* i was *home.* …*(a tear tracks down the smudge of his cheek)* …*that.* …*that's* what's *left.* the *sound* of being *run* to. …*(his eyes open, desperate)* …she *can't* have *that* one, can she? …*Vesper.* …*tell* me the *running* is *still* *mine.* …*tell* me i didn't *give* away …the *sound* of my *girl* …being *glad* i *came* *home.*

nodes.speaker: Toller
nodes.text: *(He nods vaguely, the doubt you may have planted already softening back toward the worn warm devotion, because doubt is heavy and he has so little left to hold it with.)* …*'course.* …*(he looks back toward Vesper, and the automatic smile begins to return)* …isn't she — …*(he catches himself, frowns, loses the thread)* …*come* *back*, cold one. …i had — there was a *thing* i wanted to *ask* you. something about — *(the warm empty place)* …*no.* *gone.* …*(a faint, lost smile)* …*come* *back* *anyway.* …you *look* at me like i'm still — like there's still a *whole* *man* *here* to *look* at. …*nobody's* *looked* at me like *that* …since i *started* *giving* *myself* *away.*
